title: Staff Vacancies | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Staff Vacancies
description: > Northstowe Secondary College is a brand-new secondary school at the heart of the Northstowe Education Campus, that opened to Year 7s in September 2019. Our ai
text: Staff Vacancies | Northstowe Secondary College Staff Vacancies > Northstowe Secondary College is a brand-new secondary school at the heart of the Northstowe Education Campus, that opened to Year 7s in September 2019. Our aim is to provide world class education to the developing community. As a Meridian Trust school, Northstowe Secondary College benefits from having an experienced team that is creating an inclusive, innovative and aspirational learning environment for the young people and families it serves. The College currently has students in Years 7–11.  Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Testimonials | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Testimonials
description: > We think the NSC family is a special one and we are very grateful to our families for their support, encouragement and feedback. They understand our vision to
text: Testimonials | Northstowe Secondary College Testimonials > We think the NSC family is a special one and we are very grateful to our families for their support, encouragement and feedback. They understand our vision to be open and communicate honestly. Their kindness is heart-warming and without them we wouldn’t be able to grow and respond to their needs. You can read some of our most recent testimonials below. We would love to hear from you, if you would like to add a testimonial, please email office@northstowe.education with the subject “Testimonial” Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Careers and Guidance for Parents | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Careers and Guidance for Parents
description: > 80.75% of students say their parents help them to make key careers decisions.
text: Careers and Guidance for Parents | Northstowe Secondary College Careers and Guidance for Parents > 80.75% of students say their parents help them to make key careers decisions. Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Careers and Guidance for Students | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Careers and Guidance for Students
description: > We understand what a stressful time it can be when looking at decisions that are going to affect your future, from choosing your GCSE options in Year 8 to loo
text: Careers and Guidance for Students | Northstowe Secondary College Careers and Guidance for Students > We understand what a stressful time it can be when looking at decisions that are going to affect your future, from choosing your GCSE options in Year 8 to looking at Post 16 or 18 provisions. During our students’ time at Northstowe Learning Community, they will receive a whole range of activities, sessions and experiences that will make all those occasions that little bit easier. To support our students with their career exploration, we have compiled a list of useful career websites. This list Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Careers and Guidance for Teachers | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Careers and Guidance for Teachers
description: > Northstowe Secondary College is committed to providing high quality Careers Learning to help inspire students and to enable them to make well-informed and rea
text: Careers and Guidance for Teachers | Northstowe Secondary College Careers and Guidance for Teachers > Northstowe Secondary College is committed to providing high quality Careers Learning to help inspire students and to enable them to make well-informed and realistic career decisions. We aim to meet all eight Benchmarks for a “Good Careers Guidance” as identified by the Gatsby Foundation. Our Leadership team views careers as being the responsibility of all staff in our academy. We can all make small changes in the curriculum that can have a big impact in the classrooms. We work closely with Gro Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Information for Employers | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Information for Employers
description: > At Northstowe Secondary College, we are busy growing your employees of the future, but we need your help. With your active support we can raise their career a
text: Information for Employers | Northstowe Secondary College Information for Employers > At Northstowe Secondary College, we are busy growing your employees of the future, but we need your help. With your active support we can raise their career aspirations, develop their employability skills and learn more about the exciting industries in Cambridgeshire and Peterborough. We would love to hear from parents and employers who would be willing to help build our students knowledge and experience of sectors, workplaces, labour market information, job search skills or employability skil Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Labour Market Information | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Labour Market Information
description: > What is Labour Market Information? (LMI) A key component of our careers provision is enabling our students and their parents/ carers to have access to career
text: Labour Market Information | Northstowe Secondary College Labour Market Information > What is Labour Market Information? (LMI) A key component of our careers provision is enabling our students and their parents/ carers to have access to career and LMI. This information and/or Intelligence helps provide data, statistics and predictions about present and future trends in the workplace. It also provides information on different careers, their entry routes, salaries paid, skills and qualifications needed. The Importance of LMI Keeping up to date with what’s happening in the labour  Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

text: Post-16 Pathways | Northstowe Secondary College Post-16 Pathways > Post-16 Open Evenings 2025 To keep families informed with any Post-16 provider open evenings, we will be sharing information with our families and students via email, Facebook and on this page, which will be regularly updated. label: Year 10 Work Experience
description: > Work Experience is an important part of the Year 10 Curriculum and is an excellent opportunity for young people to gain an insight into the ‘World of Work’ an
text: Year 10 Work Experience | Northstowe Secondary College Year 10 Work Experience > Work Experience is an important part of the Year 10 Curriculum and is an excellent opportunity for young people to gain an insight into the ‘World of Work’ and to help prepare them for the responsibilities and opportunities of adult life. We believe that it improves understanding of how business and industry operate, improves self-confidence, self-reliance, and maturity, and also helps students develop skills which they will need throughout their life. We know it can be one of the most transfo Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Year 8 / 9 Options | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Year 8 / 9 Options
description: > Choosing your pathway for KS4 and your GCSEs is a big decision. We are here to help you every step of the way. Each year the exact subject options adapt to pr
text: Year 8 / 9 Options | Northstowe Secondary College Year 8 / 9 Options > Choosing your pathway for KS4 and your GCSEs is a big decision. We are here to help you every step of the way. Each year the exact subject options adapt to provide the best pathway for students at that time. When it is time to request your options, you will receive a booklet detailing courses and processes. You will also have time with your tutor and subject teachers to discuss the best choices. grid_view All subjects school Key Stage 4 menu_book Key Stage 3 Key questions to discuss at home, w Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Extending the Boundaries of Learning & ‘Learning Outside the Classroom’ | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Extending the Boundaries of Learning & ‘Learning Outside the Classroom’
description: > Meridian Trust’s Vision for Extending the Boundaries of Learning: Meridian Trust provide a breadth of experience through the curriculum and beyond, including
text: Extending the Boundaries of Learning & ‘Learning Outside the Classroom’ | Northstowe Secondary College Extending the Boundaries of Learning & ‘Learning Outside the Classroom’ > Meridian Trust’s Vision for Extending the Boundaries of Learning: Meridian Trust provide a breadth of experience through the curriculum and beyond, including an entitlement for all students to experience educational visits and other activities that enhance and extend the curriculum. This includes providing a range of opportunities for spiritual, moral, social and cultural education that instill the values of the Trust and school extensively. Northstowe Secondary College fully embraces Meridian Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

label: Student Leadership
description: > Student leadership gives every young person a voice in shaping school life — through formal roles, events, and consultations. Our aim record_voice_over All me
text: Student Leadership | Northstowe Secondary College Student Leadership > Student leadership gives every young person a voice in shaping school life — through formal roles, events, and consultations. Our aim record_voice_over All members of the NSC family should have a voice in it. All voices and comments are of merit, even if they seem to criticise. However, we seek to be positive in our interactions with our students, parents, other staff and visitors. verified It is important to keep true to our values in all our interactions. diversity_3 All students should have Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

label: Access Arrangements
description: > Who are the Joint Council for Qualifications (JCQ)? JCQ is a membership organisation comprising the eight largest providers of qualifications. It provides a s
text: Access Arrangements | Northstowe Secondary College Access Arrangements > Who are the Joint Council for Qualifications (JCQ)? JCQ is a membership organisation comprising the eight largest providers of qualifications. It provides a single voice for it’s members on the issues of examination administration, providing common administration and regulations to support in reducing bureaucracy for schools and colleges. Every year, the Joint Council for Qualifications (JCQ) produce a document for Adjustments and Reasonable Adjustments. This is available online and contains t Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Curriculum | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Curriculum
description: > We want students to enjoy their learning at Northstowe, and to be proud of who they are and the things they achieve, both inside and outside the classroom. Ou
text: Curriculum | Northstowe Secondary College Curriculum > We want students to enjoy their learning at Northstowe, and to be proud of who they are and the things they achieve, both inside and outside the classroom. Our curriculum is broad, enriching and academically rigorous, and constantly seeks to stretch and inspire. We understand that all children are different and we make it our mission to know and support every one of them to become successful learners, confident individuals, and compassionate and responsible citizens. ​ Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Enrichment | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Enrichment
description: > Northstowe Secondary College is committed to providing all of our students with a wide range of additional opportunities and experiences. In this section you
text: Enrichment | Northstowe Secondary College Enrichment > Northstowe Secondary College is committed to providing all of our students with a wide range of additional opportunities and experiences. In this section you will find out information on extra-curricular activities that are currently available. Such as clubs, student leadership, trips and Duke of Edinburgh awards (DofE). There is also information our PLEDGES programme which captures and celebrates the wider experiences all of our students will participate in. Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

Please click HERE for the Exam Information Pack, which all candidates must read and understand prior to their first examination. Please note that the Exam Contingency days for 2026 are Wednesday 17 th June (all day) and Thursday 18 th June (all day) . Early Entry GCSEs Summer 2025: Languages: Some native speaking students have been identified as GCSE candidates this academic year. title: Homework (PREP) | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Homework (PREP)
description: > At Northstowe Secondary College, we call our homework ‘PREP’. We want PREP activities to complement our curriculum and provide students with meaningful opport
text: Homework (PREP) | Northstowe Secondary College Homework (PREP) > At Northstowe Secondary College, we call our homework ‘PREP’. We want PREP activities to complement our curriculum and provide students with meaningful opportunities to build fluency in literacy and numeracy, to practise their skills and retrieval of knowledge, and to extend their knowledge and passion for the subject. We believe that PREP plays a vital role in developing students’ learning, but we also recognise that having free time of evenings and weekends is equally important in supporting Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Interventions | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Interventions
description: > We aim to provide a high-quality learning environment in every classroom at Northstowe, but we recognise that some of our students will still need additional
text: Interventions | Northstowe Secondary College Interventions > We aim to provide a high-quality learning environment in every classroom at Northstowe, but we recognise that some of our students will still need additional support in order to fully access our curriculum. We want to quickly and accurately identify and support these students, and provide them with high quality intervention so that they can achieve their full potential and experience success. Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Progress and Assessment | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Progress and Assessment
description: > At Northstowe Secondary College we ensure every student can reach their full potential. The use of assessment and reporting enables us to identify a students’
text: Progress and Assessment | Northstowe Secondary College Progress and Assessment > At Northstowe Secondary College we ensure every student can reach their full potential. The use of assessment and reporting enables us to identify a students’ current progress and attainment – if they require additional support, we intervene immediately with a comprehensive intervention programme. Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Teaching and Learning Strategy | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Teaching and Learning Strategy
description: > At Northstowe, want great teaching and learning to take place in every classroom at Northstowe, so our curriculum aims can be met, and our students can fulfil
text: Teaching and Learning Strategy | Northstowe Secondary College Teaching and Learning Strategy > At Northstowe, want great teaching and learning to take place in every classroom at Northstowe, so our curriculum aims can be met, and our students can fulfil their potential. We want to draw upon evidence informed practice and research to ensure that we’re being as effective and as inclusive as possible. We want learning to transform the lives of our students, and to leave a legacy of knowledge, skills and experience that enriches our young people’s future. Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Whole School Fluency | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Whole School Fluency
description: > ‘Building Fluency’ is the first milestone in NSC’s Curriculum Roadmap. We believe that a lack of fluency in literacy and numeracy skills can be a huge barrier
text: Whole School Fluency | Northstowe Secondary College Whole School Fluency > ‘Building Fluency’ is the first milestone in NSC’s Curriculum Roadmap. We believe that a lack of fluency in literacy and numeracy skills can be a huge barrier to students’ learning, which is why we prioritise removing these barriers in our curriculum as quickly as possible so everyone can access, enjoy and achieve in our curriculum. We take a whole school approach and believe it is the responsibility of every teacher and department to incorporate strategies and resources within their teaching  Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

text: Year 11 Masterclasses | Northstowe Secondary College Year 11 Masterclasses > Below is the Year 11 masterclass schedule for 2026. Masterclasses will run up to and including the final examination on Friday 19th June. title: Absence | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Absence
description: > When should my child be absent? There may be times when your child is unable to come to school due to illness however, some types of illness do not require ab
text: Absence | Northstowe Secondary College Absence > When should my child be absent? There may be times when your child is unable to come to school due to illness however, some types of illness do not require absence from school, unless of course, your child is feeling too unwell to attend. Guidance on when to keep your child away from school can be found below: Children and young people settings: tools and resources – GOV.UK (www.gov.uk) Is my child too ill for school? – NHS (www.nhs.uk) Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Attendance | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Attendance
description: > We are pleased that children want to attend our school and that they know that good attendance matters. Students who have good attendance: get better jobs in
text: Attendance | Northstowe Secondary College Attendance > We are pleased that children want to attend our school and that they know that good attendance matters. Students who have good attendance: get better jobs in the future, achieve better results, make better friendships and have good self-esteem and confidence. We want to continue to work with you to ensure the school environment is somewhere your child wants to be and support you in ensuring they can attend every day, on time. Encouraging regular school attendance is one of the most powerful wa Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Careers (CEIAG) | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Careers (CEIAG)
description: > At Northstowe Learning Community, we believe every student should have access to real-world, relevant learning. Careers information is critical when making de
text: Careers (CEIAG) | Northstowe Secondary College Careers (CEIAG) > At Northstowe Learning Community, we believe every student should have access to real-world, relevant learning. Careers information is critical when making decisions about next steps and future choices. Our CEIAG programme is flexible and adapts as new opportunities arise. We have a dedicated team of knowledgeable professionals who help, support and teach students about the choices available. Our aim is to make sure that every student achieves their full potential. Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Catering | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Catering
description: > Students have the option of bringing in a packed lunch or purchasing food from the school canteen. Packed Lunch Students are welcome to bring a packed lunch.
text: Catering | Northstowe Secondary College Catering > Students have the option of bringing in a packed lunch or purchasing food from the school canteen. Packed Lunch Students are welcome to bring a packed lunch. Please note that we are a NUT FREE school. Please ensure that you do not so send your child to school with snacks/food that contain nuts. Students that bring a packed lunch are still able eat in the dining hall with their friends. School Canteen Our catering team works incredibly hard to offer a wide selection of hot and cold food at both Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

label: Celebrating Achievement
description: > At Northstowe Secondary College, we love to celebrate the amazing things that our students do and recognise their achievements. Here are just a few of our stu
text: Celebrating Achievement | Northstowe Secondary College Celebrating Achievement > At Northstowe Secondary College, we love to celebrate the amazing things that our students do and recognise their achievements. Here are just a few of our students’ incredible achievements: On Sunday the 5th of September, Northstowe had their first ever running festival and despite the extremely hot weather many runners took part and completed their events. It was great to see so many of Northstowe Secondary College students taking part as well as many more cheering along the course. There was Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: EAL | Northstowe Secondary College
label: EAL
description: > English as an additional language (EAL) is taken very seriously at Northstowe Secondary College. Our aim is to support and develop a pupil’s ability to commun
text: EAL | Northstowe Secondary College EAL > English as an additional language (EAL) is taken very seriously at Northstowe Secondary College. Our aim is to support and develop a pupil’s ability to communicate effectively in a language which is not their mother tongue – enabling pupils to undertake their studies in all of their subject areas successfully. You can find out more information on how we do this below. Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: House System | Northstowe Secondary College
label: House System
description: > Our House System reflects the structure and systems that have been instrumental the success of Meridian Trust Secondary schools and creates a strong team and
text: House System | Northstowe Secondary College House System > Our House System reflects the structure and systems that have been instrumental the success of Meridian Trust Secondary schools and creates a strong team and community identity. It reflects and delivers the commitment to ‘know, value and support every student to achieve.’ The College is divided into four Houses, with form groups of about 21 students. The intention is to have more houses as the school grows. The Houses provide support and guidance within an environment where the needs of each c Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Library | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Library
description: > The library is designed to provide for the students a quiet, comfortable, and safe area suitable for effective study or quiet, independent reading and is open
text: Library | Northstowe Secondary College Library > The library is designed to provide for the students a quiet, comfortable, and safe area suitable for effective study or quiet, independent reading and is open to students at break and lunch. Break times in the library are for quiet independent reading and homework whereas lunchtimes can see a range of clubs and activities including chess club, mindfulness colouring, and other games available for small groups of students to have fun with. The library is housed on the first floor with a view ont Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

text: Pastoral Care and Safeguarding | Northstowe Secondary College Pastoral Care and Safeguarding > The Internet has become an integral part of children’s lives, enabling them to undertake research for school projects, talk to their friends and access information from around the world. text: Rewards and Sanctions | Northstowe Secondary College Rewards and Sanctions > At Northstowe Secondary College, we work together to keep a pleasant, purposeful atmosphere where every student lives our values: kind , curious and hardworking . text: SEND | Northstowe Secondary College SEND > Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) — Northstowe Secondary College is committed and passionate about ensuring ‘Achievement for All’, by setting high aspirations and providing a high quality teaching and learning environment, for all students. However, we acknowledge that some students require additional support, for part or all of their time with us, to enable them to reach their full potential. NSC has a dedicated team who strive to provide the best provision for students with s Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Travel | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Travel
description: > Northstowe Secondary College was built to serve the local community and the intention is that all students within our catchment area of Northstowe and Longsta
text: Travel | Northstowe Secondary College Travel > Northstowe Secondary College was built to serve the local community and the intention is that all students within our catchment area of Northstowe and Longstanton will be able to either walk or cycle to College. As a result, there is no College transport available for any students at Northstowe. If you have applied for your child to attend NSC and live out of our catchment area, the Local Authority will expect you to make your own arrangements for your child to get to and from College for the  Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Uniform | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Uniform
description: > At the college, we have clear expectations of uniform and appearance. Our school uniform assists in the development of a unified culture and conveys a strong
text: Uniform | Northstowe Secondary College Uniform > At the college, we have clear expectations of uniform and appearance. Our school uniform assists in the development of a unified culture and conveys a strong message about our standards in school and the community. Students must be impeccably presented in the correct school uniform at all times. The NSC uniform consists of a navy blazer, tie (with a house colour), white shirt or blouse and dark grey trousers. The school only allows one style of skirt and grey suit style trousers. All socks and Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Admissions | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Admissions
description: > As an Academy, the Academy Council is responsible for establishing the Admission Criteria and oversubscription criteria for the College; the Academy Council a
text: Admissions | Northstowe Secondary College Admissions > As an Academy, the Academy Council is responsible for establishing the Admission Criteria and oversubscription criteria for the College; the Academy Council agreed to follow the admissions and oversubscription criteria set by Cambs County Council. These criteria have not been changed by the Academy Council following the establishment of the Academy. The responsibility for the administration of the Admissions process remains with the Local Authority. text: Applying for Free School Meals | Northstowe Secondary College Applying for Free School Meals > To apply for Free School Meals click here . title: Bromcom/MCAS | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Bromcom/MCAS
description: > We would like to introduce you to our parental information and communication system; Bromcom and MCAS (My Child at School). We are keen to maintain good and i
text: Bromcom/MCAS | Northstowe Secondary College Bromcom/MCAS > We would like to introduce you to our parental information and communication system; Bromcom and MCAS (My Child at School). We are keen to maintain good and immediate communication between families and school. We think that this system will help to improve this further by letting you have instant information about your child, these include: Their timetable Attendance Behaviour Dinner money top ups Recent meals Homework Upcoming clubs and trips Outstanding payments Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Early Closure Notification | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Early Closure Notification
description: > School closures happen because of an emergency such as severe weather. In the rare event that we have to close due to snow, we aim to make a decision by 7.30a
text: Early Closure Notification | Northstowe Secondary College Early Closure Notification > School closures happen because of an emergency such as severe weather. In the rare event that we have to close due to snow, we aim to make a decision by 7.30am. Early morning closure If there is any reason that the school cannot open in the morning, the school will do the following: Email parents and carers through Bromcom/MCAS, please be aware that due to demand on the software, messages take time to get through. Post on the school website. Post on our Facebook page. Alternatively, you can ch Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

title: Home School Communication | Northstowe Secondary College
label: Home School Communication
description: > Honest and open communication is key to the success of your child. It is only by working together that we can help your child achieve their dreams. We don’t w
text: Home School Communication | Northstowe Secondary College Home School Communication > Honest and open communication is key to the success of your child. It is only by working together that we can help your child achieve their dreams. We don’t want to put ceilings on children’s achievement and need your support to help them succeed. It is our goal to communicate with you honestly and with integrity. If we get it wrong, we apologise; when we get it right, we celebrate — and the children come first in all we do. favorite Kind lightbulb Curious trending_up Hard working We expect al Home About Learning NSC Life Parents and Carers News and Events

text: Keeping Children Safe Online | Northstowe Secondary College Keeping Children Safe Online > Meridian Trust’s E-Safety Policy can be found here . Being online is an integral part of children and young people’s lives. Social media, online games, websites and apps can be accessed through mobile phones, computers, laptops and tablets – all of which form a part of children and young people’s online world, enabling them to undertake research for school projects, talk to their friends and access information from around the world. text: Safeguarding Statement | Northstowe Secondary College Safeguarding Statement > Under section 175 of the Education Act 2002 (as amended), *the Education (Independent School Standards) Regulations 2014, the Non-Maintained Special Schools (England) Regulations 2015, and the Apprenticeships, Skills, Children and Learning Act 2009 (as amended) to have arrangements in place to safeguard and promote the welfare of children.
